messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Error: (sendFailed) Error: Request session.create failed with message: Model "copilotcli/gpt-5.4" is not available. ISSUE BODY: Testing #318183 I get the following error when using model "Auto": ``` Error: (sendFailed) Error: Request session.create failed with message: Model "copilotcli/gpt-5.4" is not available. ``` Then I try a different model and get the following error: ``` Error: (sendFailed) Error: git worktree exited with code 255: fatal: a branch named 'agents/translate-the-readme-to-french-82551f6c' already exists ``` --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- #### Copilot CLI terminal (independent) ``` invoke_agent (CLIENT) ← standalone copilot binary │ service.name = github-copilot ├── chat gpt-4o (CLIENT) └── (independent root traces, no extension link) ``` --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### 4-C. `_sendRequest` (line ~330) — new signature + body builder ```typescript private async _sendRequest( githubToken: string, request: Anthropic.MessageCreateParamsBase, stream: boolean, options?: ICopilotApiServiceRequestOptions, ): Promise { ``` **Body construction** — spread the SDK-shaped request, inject `stream`, normalize `system`: ```typescript const { system, ...rest } = request; const body = JSON.stringify({ ...rest, stream, // CAPI requires system as a text-block array, not a raw string ...(system !== undefined ? { system: typeof system === 'string' ? [{ type: 'text', text: system }] : system } : {}), }); ``` **Headers** — merge caller headers first so security-sensitive ones always win: ```typescript headers: { ...options?.headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'Authorization': `Bearer ${copilotToken}`, 'X-Request-Id': requestId, 'OpenAI-Intent': 'conversation', }, signal: options?.signal, ``` Replace all internal `signal` references with `options?.signal`. Pass `options?.signal` to `_getCopilotToken`. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Copilot CLI (Terminal Session) Terminal CLI sessions ("New Copilot CLI Session") run as a separate process. When OTel is enabled, the extension forwards `COPILOT_OTEL_ENABLED` and `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` to the terminal process. Terminal traces appear as **independent root traces** (service `github-copilot`) — they are not linked to extension traces. > **Note:** The CLI runtime only supports `otlp-http`. When `otlp-grpc` is configured, the terminal CLI still uses HTTP. Backends that serve both protocols on the same port (e.g., Aspire Dashboard) work transparently. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- | `copilot_chat.debug_name` | When available | `agentMode` | | `error.type` | On error | `TimeoutError` | | `gen_ai.input.messages` | Opt-in (captureContent) | `[{"role":"system",...}]` | | `gen_ai.system_instructions` | Opt-in (captureContent) | `[{"type":"text",...}]` | **`execute_tool`** — one span per tool invocation (span kind: `INTERNAL`). | Attribute | Requirement | Example | |---|---|---| | `gen_ai.operation.name` | Required | `execute_tool` | | `gen_ai.tool.name` | Required | `readFile` | | `gen_ai.tool.type` | Required | `function` or `extension` (MCP tools) | | `gen_ai.tool.call.id` | Recommended | `call_abc123` | | `gen_ai.tool.description` | When available | `Read the contents of a file` | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.edit_type` | Edit tools | `create` \| `update` \| `str_replace` \| `insert` | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.skill_name` | When invoking a skill | `auto-perf-optimize` | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.mcp_server_name_hash` | MCP tools | SHA-256 hex of server name | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.mcp_tool_name` | MCP tools | `search_issues` | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.command` | Shell tools, opt-in (captureContent) | `npm test` (truncated to 256 chars) | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.file_path` | File tools, opt-in (captureContent) | `/src/app.ts` | | `github.copilot.tool.parameters.mcp_server_name` | MCP tools, opt-in (captureContent) | `github` | | `error.type` | On error | `FileNotFoundError` | | `gen_ai.tool.call.arguments` | Opt-in (captureContent) | `{"filePath":"/src/index.ts"}` | | `gen_ai.tool.call.result` | Opt-in (captureContent) | `(file contents or summary)` | --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## 1. Context ### What ships before this phase **Phase 1 (done):** `ICopilotApiService` at `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/shared/copilotApiService.ts`. A gateway to GitHub Copilot's chat completions API ("**CAPI**") that exposes: ```ts interface ICopilotApiService { readonly _serviceBrand: undefined; messages(githubToken: string, body: Anthropic.MessageCreateParamsNonStreaming, options?: ICopilotApiServiceRequestOptions): Promise; messages(githubToken: string, body: Anthropic.MessageCreateParamsStreaming, options?: ICopilotApiServiceRequestOptions): AsyncGenerator; countTokens(githubToken: string): Promise; // throws — CAPI does not support this models(githubToken: string): Promise; } interface ICopilotApiServiceRequestOptions { headers?: Record; signal?: AbortSignal; } ``` `messages()` is a TypeScript overload — streaming returns an `AsyncGenerator` of pre-parsed `Anthropic.MessageStreamEvent` objects; non-streaming returns a `Promise`. CAPI is wirecompatible with the Anthropic Messages API. `ICopilotApiService` is hand-rolled HTTP (not the `@anthropic-ai/sdk` client) and currently throws plain `Error` on both non-2xx and SSE error events. **Q10's passthrough design depends on widening this contract — see §1.5.** **Phase 1.5 (done):** widened `ICopilotApiService` to use raw `Anthropic.MessageStreamEvent` types and accept `ICopilotApiServiceRequestOptions { headers?, signal? }`.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Handle unavailable models and git branch conflicts gracefully in Copilot Chat",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "copilot-chat", "error-handling"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a developer using Copilot Chat, I want the system to handle unavailable models and git branch conflicts gracefully with fallback mechanisms and clear error messages so that I can continue working without being blocked by transient model issues or git operations.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given the user selects 'Auto' mode, when the default model is unavailable, then display a clear error message explaining the issue and suggest manual model selection",
    "Given a model request fails due to unavailability, when retrying with an alternative available model, then successfully complete the chat session",
    "Given the agent attempts to create a git worktree for a new branch, when a branch already exists, then either use existing branch or generate unique branch name and succeed",
    "Given any API request fails, when error occurs, then provide structured error information including model name, error type, and suggested actions"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Add model availability check in ICopilotApiService before attempting to use 'Auto' mode",
      "Implement fallback mechanism when primary model is unavailable",
      "Enhance error handling in _sendRequest to capture and propagate specific error types (model not found, git conflicts)",
      "Add retry logic for transient failures with exponential backoff",
      "Update models() method to include availability status or handle unknown models gracefully"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Improve error display in Copilot Chat UI to show model-specific errors and fallback suggestions",
      "Add user-friendly message when git branch creation fails, suggesting alternative approaches",
      "Display available models list with their current availability status",
      "Show actionable error messages with suggested next steps"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test 'Auto' mode behavior when default model is unavailable",
      "Verify fallback mechanism works correctly across different scenarios",
      "Test git branch conflict handling for multiple concurrent agents",
      "Validate error messages are clear and actionable for end users",
      "Confirm retry logic handles transient failures appropriately"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by team lead",
    "All unit tests passing",
    "Integration tests covering model fallback scenarios",
    "E2E tests verifying error handling in various conditions",
    "Documentation updated with new error handling behavior"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected fallback strategy when 'Auto' mode cannot find any available model?",
    "Should we cache model availability status to reduce API calls?",
    "Are there specific models that are known to be unavailable in certain regions/organizations?",
    "How should we handle cases where all configured models are temporarily unavailable?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The ICopilotApiService already has access to model information via the models() method",
    "Git branch conflicts occur when multiple agents try to create branches simultaneously",
    "Users may not always have internet connectivity or Copilot subscription active",
    "The current implementation throws plain Error objects that need better type safety"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Model availability detection and fallback",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Implement checking if models are available before use, with automatic fallback to alternative models"
    },
    {
      "name": "Git branch conflict resolution",
      "priority": "medium",
      "description": "Handle cases where git branches already exist when agents try to create new ones"
    },
    {
      "name": "Enhanced error messaging",
      "priority": "low",
      "description": "Improve user-facing error messages with actionable suggestions"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {
      "story": "Model availability detection and fallback",
      "points": 5,
      "estimated_days": 3
    },
    {
      "story": "Git branch conflict resolution",
      "points": 3,
      "estimated_days": 2
    },
    {
      "story": "Enhanced error messaging",
      "points": 2,
      "estimated_days": 1
    }
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - _sendRequest implementation details",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Copilot CLI terminal session documentation",
    "Issue body showing two distinct error scenarios"
  ]
}
```
